question_text: A student rubs a polythene rod with a cloth. The rod becomes negatively charged. What can you conclude about the cloth after this process?
correct_answer: The cloth becomes positively charged (or has lost electrons)
explanation: When the polythene rod gains electrons (becoming negative), those electrons must come from the cloth. This leaves the cloth with fewer electrons, so it becomes positively charged. Charge is conserved—one object gains the charge the other loses.
